title: Level 696
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 696: "A Rainy Day"}}{{Class 0|entity=Devoid of harmful Entities}} {{Blurimage|file=Parkatnight.png|hovertext=The center of this place?|caption=An image taken by a wanderer showing the center of the level.|width=350px}} {{Background|file=NIGHT SUBURBAN.png}} == Description == '''Level 696''', also known as A Rainy Day, is an expansive nature park which appears to stretch approximately 20 miles in all directions. Upon appearing in this level, one of the first things wanderers notice is a dirt path going in all 4 directions: north, south, east, and west. After walking in any direction for about two hundred and fifty meters, one will encounter a bus stop. Level 696 has some unusual properties, such as the fact that it is always night. It is also always raining. However, the rain never stays on the ground for any longer than an hour. Wanderers have tried to contain the water within cups, buckets, and basins. Despite managing to contain it, the water evaporated within less than an hour. It is unknown what causes the water to evaporate at such a quick pace, as the temperature in this level ranges from 15°C to 22°C. The wind in this level averages at 20-30 Mph. ==== The Level 696 effect ==== The level 696 effect has a wide range of different effects on wanderers, ranging from mild to extreme. All affected wanderers state feeling an extreme sense of safety, with others reporting feeling lightheaded while looking at the sky, though unable to look away. The worst reports are all of wanderers reporting an urge to stay outside and look at the stars forever. Wanderers in this state have been reported to do nothing but fulfill basic survival needs and stare at the sky. This effect appears unbroken during the brightening and strengthened during the Lunar eclipse. It has been reported the only ways to snap wanderers out of this state is by getting them indoors. This effect is the strongest outside and fades the further from the center of the level one is. The main methods of countering it are entering a house, drinking almond water, or getting away from the center of the level ==== '''Trance''' ==== This level has a trance effect which appears to calm wanderers while causing entities to act less aggressively, in some cases, wanderers report entities acting completely docile.{{Blurimage|hovertext=The inside of the bus.|file=BUSNIGHT.jpg|caption=An image showing the interior of the bus.}} Scattered throughout the level, one will encounter bus stops approximately every 15 minutes. Upon reaching one of these bus stops, one may be required to wait up to an hour, as the bus drives around the entirety of the level in approximately 3 hours, though somehow arriving at all bus stops within half an hour of waiting. The interior of the bus is well-lit with comfortable seats and a cold, marble-like floor.. The driver of the bus is a [[Entity 9|Faceling]]. The bus is a 2-story-bus with public Wi-Fi available at all times. Every 7-8 hours, The driver will temporarily stop driving to get up and hand out [[Object 1|almond water]] and food. The bus has speakers on the roof, playing songs at all times. Despite the seats being soft, the appear almost indestructible. The bus will drive on the concrete paths all around the level. There have been few occasions of the driver [[Entity 9|Faceling]] leaving the bus for a few minutes at an undocumented area with cobble paths and short castle-like buildings. There have been a handful of occasions where the bus was reported to have crashed, though it appears to be exceptionally rare. The first reported case was August 2019. There are three types of weather on this level: '''Rain, Sunny''', and '''Stormy'''. ==== '''Rain''' ==== This is the default weather on this level. The rainwater is always at a comfortable temperature, despite the drinking water being freezing. If out of drinking water, it is highly recommended to grab some of the rainwater off the ground and freeze it. It will not disappear if frozen. From there, one may unfreeze the water. ==== '''Sunny''' ==== This is the weather that occurs during the brightening event. during this, the wind will come to a near-complete stop. additionally, this weather will cause the level to increase drastically in heat.. If caught in this weather, especially during the rare Scorching Inferno event, skin will likely dry out within an hour. If one is caught outside during this weather, one should seek shade or get in one of the pools at '''The Mansion on the Hill.''' ==== '''Stormy''' ==== This type of weather is typically only reported once a year. It is the rarest and most dangerous weather type. The wind increases to 90 mph, and the rain will increase in amount and speed. Lightning will strike. The lightning is not fatal. The most severe effect of being struck is memory scrambling. Any wanderers within a 100 ft radius of any struck individual may have their memories scrambled with any other nearby wanderer. == Areas == ===== Outdoors ===== This area is the safest and largest area in the level. It is estimated to take up around 90% of the level, In this area, one may encounter several different types of trees. One will commonly stumble across sidewalk-like concrete paths, despite the absence of any roads. while walking along these paths, one will typically encounter a Wooden bench and a metal bin every 50-100 meters. Wanderers report that damaging one bench causes all other benches within a 2 mile radius to be damaged, although all benches seem to reform within approximately 2 hours of damage. ===== '''Suburban''' ===== In the suburban areas of the level, there are many rows of houses, each street having different coloured houses. Houses painted black are completely safe. Houses painted green are safe. However, despite looking normal from the outside, on the inside, they are inverted. It is advised to enter these houses with caution, as the inversion effect starts instantly upon entry. Meaning one will be rotated onto the roof.Houses painted yellow are somewhat safe, though these housed always have some form of damage. Reports vary from reporting sinkholes, to a punch mark in the wall. Houses painted red are heavily damaged and unstable, making them unsafe to live in. Wanderers have reported floor tiles giving out and dropping into an abyss. Other reports state the roof tiles have fallen in X-shape patterns. Rain mat additionally leak through the ceiling. Some wanderers who braved the harsh living conditions report hearing unusual sounds from the attics these houses have. These are the only houses with an attic. One of the first things one may notice upon approaching the houses is a stone path leading up to the house. Straying off this path has been documented to cause one's vision to temporarily blur and distort, though it has been reported to fade within 5-10 minutes and has not been documented to cause permanent damage. At each side of the path, a patch of grass littered with small trees, and bushes lays. The majority of wanderers report fully furnished interiors. Throughout the house one may encounter plant pots scattered in the corners of most rooms. Upon entering a house, one will find themselves staring at a coat hanger. Looking right will result in one finding a hallway leading to a staircase, a living room, and a bathroom. The floor of the hallway is typically a carpet like material, despite feeling like snow. The floor appears to stay at a consistent temperature of 3°C. The walls appear to be out of a metal plating, which reports prove to be near-indestructible. The bathroom floor is made out of white ceramic tiles. The room consists of a toilet, a bath, a towel hanger, a sink, and a flickering light bulb. Inside the living room, one will be greeted with multiple couches, a desk with a singular pen left out, and a shelf containing an assortment of different items in it. Attempting to open the shelf may cause a hidden lever to materialise. Activating this lever causes an extra staircase to appear, leading down into a long hallway. This long hallway leads to a metallic door. Opening the door reveals a fully furnished room. The room comes filled with a couch, an old television, and a tiled floor. If one were to choose to go upstairs, one will be greeted with an equally long, but less dusty hallway.{{Blurimage|file=Attic.jpeg|hovertext=Do not enter.|caption=An image of the attic.|width=350px}} This hallway has one door on each side of it, and one at the end. The two at each side lead to a bedroom, while the one at the end opens to a bathroom. In red houses, one of the roof tiles will be replaced with a wooden trapdoor. It may be opened if one were to insert a key into it. Wanderers have reported finding the yellow key in cabinets downstairs. However, it should be noted that wanderers have also reported it disappearing, despite them having it in their pocket five minutes prior. Inside the attic, wooden walls and floors have been documented. Furthermore, a light illuminates the room from an unknown source. It shines brightest on an old rocking chair in the middle of the room. Documented evidence suggests the rocking chair is impossible to dismantle. It is theorized whatever makes all the noises up there is capable of invisibility, as if one were to enter the attic, one would not find the culprit of the noises. The houses also seem to have some unusual properties, as the houses seem to be almost spotless, no matter how much mess is made. The bins also appear to empty out every hour on the dot. ===== '''Garden''' ===== Every one of the houses has a back garden filled with vegetation. A majority of gardens contain a greenhouse. Inside them, one may find a multitude of different plant life. The first type of plant one may encounter in the greenhouse are potted plants. These plants are safe to touch and have no known anomalous properties. The second type one may find are long, stemmy plants which appear to be coated in an unknown substance. Touching these plants will result in one losing the ability to move for up to 10 minutes. Consumption of the substance covering these plants may cause one to feel lightheaded, and pass out. Finally, the most common type of plants in the greenhouse are mutated versions of everyday fruits and veg. Reports indicate they are edible. However, consumption of any fruit in this area is strongly discouraged, as they may cause unintended side effects. As of now, there are only 2 documented effects one may experience upon consumption of any fruit. * 1. Vomiting. The mutated Apples are the most likely to cause this. * 2. Acute Cranial pain. This typically wears off after approximately 30 minutes. Mutated Melons have been reported to cause this effect most often. ===== '''The Mansion on the Hill''' ===== This area of the level can be found by wandering about 5 miles away from the centre of the level. Cameras seem to glitch and distort while within a 500 ft radius of the mansion, making getting a good video of this place almost impossible. The exterior of the mansion appears light brown with tinted windows. The mansions height is approximately 35 meters tall. Upon following a stone path up the hill, one will encounter an unlocked glass door. This door appears to be unlocked often. There is a pool at each side of the entrance, surrounded by hedges. The pools have ceramic tiles around them. Upon entering the mansion, one will first encounter the homeowner of the mansion, who appears to be waiting for visitors. He does not speak and has been reported only to answer yes or no questions. Each room has a card note with the words "'''HOUSE RULES'''". On the note, the rules are stated as "'''HOUSE RULES''' # Leave the lights on at all times. # DO NOT Engage in any form of combat. # Do not cause any intentional damage.". It is unknown if this was written by the Homeowner or one of their many guards, though what is known is that breaking any of these rules will result in one being escorted by one of the guards. Attempting to resist will result in one to suddenly pass out, waking up shortly after at the center of the level. The mansion has 2 different variants of pools, each covering half of the 8 mansion pools. Variant 1 is light tiled pools. These pools will have white, and light grey tiles. Pools with these colour tiles are extremely clean and have a depth of under 2 meters. Variant 2 is dark tiled pools. These pools have black, dark grey, and dark green tiles, Indicating the pool, while still likely clean, has a depth of over 3 meters. {{Blurimage|hovertext=On their own.|caption=One of the parks.|file=Swing park at night.png|width=350px}} ===== '''Swing Park''' ===== These are the smallest and by far the rarest areas. The swing park areas average a size of 250 m². Every wanderer who has encountered a swing park has recounted a strong sense of nostalgia which appears to last up to 2 hours after leaving the parks. the entrance to these areas is typically through a yellow plastic gate. Grey and yellow fences have been reported to surround the parks, with any attempts to climb over the fences proving ineffective, as wanderers report being teleported to the entrance gate. The equipment inside these parks typically consist of tall slides of varying colours, 2 pairs of swings, and a roundabout. Larger variants of these parks can be found but are exceptionally rare. Entities don't seem to be able to enter this area, making it extremely safe. Every approximately 48 hours, a thick layer fog has been reported to blanket the Swing Park areas specifically. The fog seems to keep the swing park area cool. The air appears to have a smell of chlorine. One wanderer reported seeing a pair of green eyes in the fog once, which vanished within a few seconds, making it unconfirmed if it was a one-off hallucination or something real. There are 2 known versions of this area. The normal one, and the giant swing park. Giant swing parks are reported to have climbing frames, tube slides, net swings, and much more. The sky retains its night time state during the brightening for unknown reasons. == Events == ==== The Brightening ==== Every 6 months, the levels sky turns to day for about 12 hours. During that period, no one can noclip in or out, and the temperature will increase from normal, to a range of 28°C to 35°C. The rain will also stop, and all doors will close, meaning anyone inside a building is locked in. Any wanderers who aren't inside a house may get an extreme urge to find shade or leave the level. There is a 5-minute transition in and out. During the transition, doors will start closing, lights will turn off, the temperature will start to increase, the rain will slow down until stopping, and the sky will turn from black to yellow-orange. The safest place to be is indoors. Out of the 11 documented cases of "The Brightening", 2 have been reported to be different. These 2 were nicknamed "Scorching Infernos". During the Scorching Inferno events, several differences may be noticed. It is much warmer, Averaging 45°C to 52°C. Another reported difference is the sky glowing bright orange and red, with a thin layer of blue in swing parks. ==== '''Lunar Eclipse''' ==== It is unknown how often this occurs, as there have only ever been 3 reported cases of this event ever occurring. Not much is known about the '''Lunar Eclipse.''' What is known is the moon will eclipse the entire sky, leaving nothing but blackness. When this happens, certain entities can spawn. All lights and electronics will stop working until the lunar eclipse is over. It is not recommended to stay indoors during this event, if one is inside a building, evacuation is recommended. The temperature will plummet to as low as -5°C during this event. Reports say meteors have been seen falling from the sky. They land in identical positions every time. However, they seem to cause little to no harm. ==== '''Partial''' '''Lunar Eclipse''' ==== These are a safer, more common version of the '''Lunar Eclipse'''. Happening on average once every 7-9 months, this variant of the Lunar Eclipse has some major differences, such as it being impossible for entities to appear, an absence of raining meteors, and the temperature being much warmer. The lowest recorded temperature during this event is 7°C. However, reports of this event have only started in 2022. Some electronics and devices will stay on during this event. Despite being rare, there have been numerous reports. ==== '''The Enlightened Eclipse''' ==== It is theorised that if a '''Lunar Eclipse''' and '''The Brightening''' overlap, it would create an extremely dangerous event known as '''The Enlightened Eclipse'''. Where the sky would turn a blinding white, and the temperature would go from one extreme to the other. If this event were to ever occur, the level could take years to recover from any damage. == Entities == Friendly [[Entity 9|Facelings]] can appear throughout this level. During the lunar eclipse event, [[Entity 3|Smilers]] and [[Entity 8|Hounds]] can appear extremely rarely. There have only ever been 2 documented cases of this happening. ==Colonies/Groups== ===== '''The Red Housers''' ===== '''The Red Housers''', as their name suggests, live in the dangerous red houses on this level. They are a group of 26 wanderers who dedicate their time to repairing the red houses and making them habitable. '''The Red Housers''' group typically paint the renovated red houses pink to show they are safe to live in. Their group was founded in 2022 by their leader, Jim. They have survival stashes that consist of med-kits, flashlights, walkie-talkies, and radars. They will lend gear to wanderers in need, and along with '''The Cult of the Night,''' wrote a book titled "the wanderers' guide to surviving Level 696". ===== '''The Cult of the Night''' ===== '''The Cult of the Night''' consists of 8 wanderers who refuse to ever go indoors and are responsible for most documentation of this level. They are always observing for any anomalies or anything new. The exact date the cult was founded remains a mystery. However, evidence suggests their cult was founded prior to 2019. They are always in dark clothing. They rarely accept new members but are welcoming to all wanderers and will hand out resources if requested. They are allies of the Red Housers, both working to make the level safer than it already is. ===== '''The Campers''' ===== '''The Campers''' are a group of 80 wanderers who spend the majority of their time camping for weeks or on the move. They specialize in exploring and making tents, being able to make them out of almost anything within a timespan on 12 hours-5 days. They are always open for new members. They are responsible for finding and documenting the mansion on the hills. ==Entrances and Exits== === Entrances === *Going through a certain door in [[The Hub]] will lead one to this Level. *Using a swing at night in any outdoors level has a small chance to transport one here. === Exits === *Going into any house has an extremely low chance to transport one to [[Level 9]]. *If the bus crashes while one is on it, one may be transported to [[Level 22]] most of the time. On rare occasions, one may be sent to [[The Void]]. *Falling through the floor of a red house will bring one to [[Level 6]], or, on rare occasions, [[The Void]]. *Reaching the bottom of a dark tiled pool has a small chance to cause one to noclip to [[Level 7]] or [[Level 37.1]]. *Reaching the bottom of a lightly tiled pool has a small chance to cause one to noclip to [[Level 37|Level 37.]] *Finding a giant park can cause the level to transition to [[Level 696.1]]{{Audio|file=Rain-sounds-ambience-351115.mp3|volume=0.6}} {{Collapse|num=2|initial='''> Open Author Information'''|final='''> Close Author Information'''|body='''Author''': [[User:Panda Yay|Panda Yay]]
All of the pictures have been verified with multiple AI checkers and are non-AI. I used quillbot AI to help spell-check, however everything on this page is completely my concept and was written entirely by me.}} {{Redirect|Prev=695|Next=697}} [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:Cluster I]] [[Category:Safe]]
